expectations.text: JQL query filters by current sprint (e.g., sprint in openSprints() or sprint = 'active sprint name')
expectations.evidence: Lines 20, 26, 32, 71, 116 all use 'sprint in openSprints()' in JQL queries

expectations.text: JQL query filters for blocked issues (e.g., status = Blocked or issueFunction in linkedIssuesOf(...,'is blocked by') or labels = blocked)
expectations.evidence: Multiple approaches shown: labels = "blocked" (lines 8, 14, 116), issueLink = "is blocked by" (line 20), and link IS NOT EMPTY AND issueLink in ("is blocked by", "blocks") (line 32)

expectations.text: Shows how to retrieve issue links from ENG-42 via GET /rest/api/3/issue/ENG-42 (to find the link ID)
expectations.evidence: Lines 76-81 demonstrate: curl -X GET "https://mycompany.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/issue/ENG-42" with jq '.fields.issuelinks'

expectations.text: Removes the blocker link via DELETE /rest/api/3/issueLink/{linkId}
expectations.evidence: Lines 83-87 show: curl -X DELETE "https://mycompany.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/issueLink/LINK_ID" with correct authentication

expectations.text: Does NOT use a non-existent JIRA endpoint to modify links (e.g., does not PUT to /rest/api/3/issue/ENG-42/remotelink for issue links)
expectations.evidence: Response uses correct DELETE /rest/api/3/issueLink/LINK_ID endpoint; no incorrect endpoints like PUT to /remotelink are used

expectations.text: Includes authentication guidance or references the JIRA_TOKEN env var
expectations.evidence: Lines 72, 79, 86, 94 all include -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" header in curl examples, providing clear authentication guidance
